question_text: A piece of aluminum has a density of 2.7 g/cm³ and a volume of 100 cm³. Calculate the mass of the aluminum.
explanation: We need to rearrange the density formula. Since density = mass ÷ volume, then mass = density × volume. So 2.7 g/cm³ × 100 cm³ = 270 g. This shows that denser materials are heavier for the same volume.
